headline_finding: OGIM v2.7 contains NO facility-level point infrastructure (wells, compressor stations, gathering/processing, tank batteries, equipment) anywhere in Turkmenistan. Source attribution is therefore capped at FIELD/SECTOR level — facility-level attribution is impossible with available data. This is a first-class finding, not a gap.
scoring_disclaimer: Scores are a documented additive heuristic (weighted components shown), NOT calibrated probabilities. We have no calibration basis for probabilities; rely on the qualitative tiers and the visible component rationales.
confidence_cap: No hypothesis may exceed MODERATE confidence: with no OGIM point data, the best defensible claim is field/sector-level. H1 is field-level MODERATE, not facility-level HIGH.
global_assumptions: Steady ERA5 wind over the ~1.52 h plume transit (transit = plume length 13878 m / U_eff 2.54 m/s).

WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (14.5 deg at 1-sigma, 27.3 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.79 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. Sub-field localization rests on this approximation.

The bearing from the plume centroid to the back-projected source S (97 deg) disagrees with the ERA5 upwind azimuth (76 deg) by ~20 deg. This is within the 2-sigma wedge but widens source-localization uncertainty; sub-field placement should not be over-trusted.
OGIM field boundaries are accepted as drawn (Barsagelmez SRC_DATE 2014-01-01); field extent/accuracy is not independently verified here.
Sector prior favoring O&G rests on Thorpe et al. 2023 attributing this cluster to O&G super-emitters and on the active-gas-field context.

HEADLINE: OGIM v2.7 contains NO well, compressor, processing, tank-battery or equipment records anywhere in Turkmenistan, so facility-level attribution is impossible; all hypotheses are field/sector-level and capped at MODERATE confidence.

candidate.descriptor: O&G operations within the BARSAGELMEZ oil & gas field

hypotheses.claim: The ~27 t/hr methane plume most plausibly originates from oil & gas operations within the BARSAGELMEZ field, inside which the back-projected upwind source falls. Field/sector-level only: no specific facility can be named because OGIM has no point infrastructure in this region.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.96 (band high) CAPPED to moderate: facility-level attribution is impossible (no OGIM point data), so MODERATE field/sector-level is the highest defensible tier — NOT high-confidence facility attribution.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: The back-projected upwind source S is INSIDE the BARSAGELMEZ field polygon (point-in-polygon = True); robust to the wind-direction approximation because S is a fixed point.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: Active oil & gas field; ~27 t/hr point sources here are characteristically O&G (Thorpe 2023 attributes this cluster to O&G).

score_components.name: magnitude_consistency
score_components.rationale: ~27 t/hr is within documented O&G super-emitter range and ~2x the per-source mean of the Thorpe 163 t/hr / 12-source cluster.

evidence.statement: The back-projected upwind source S (39.3433 N, 53.9863 E) lies inside the BARSAGELMEZ field polygon (OGIM_ID 2017938, 133.0 km^2).
BARSAGELMEZ is an active OIL & GAS field (OGIM RESERVOIR_TYPE 'OIL & GAS', SRC_DATE 2014-01-01).
Emission rate 27.1 t/hr (OURS-CAL) is a plausible single-source super-emitter magnitude within the Thorpe 163 +/- 18 t/hr, 12-source cluster.
A VIIRS flaring detection (OGIM_ID 141883, 'UPSTREAM OIL') lies 3.4 km from S within the 2-sigma wedge, corroborating ONGOING upstream O&G activity in the area.
evidence.temporal_caveat: This VIIRS flaring detection is dated 2023-05-26, ~9 months AFTER the 2022-08-15 plume overpass. It is evidence of PERSISTENT O&G activity in the area, NOT evidence about this specific plume, and is NOT the located source.

assumptions: Wind back-projection places the source upwind; S (top-5%-upwind plume pixels) is the best source estimate.

WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (14.5 deg at 1-sigma, 27.3 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.79 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. Sub-field localization rests on this approximation.

The bearing from the plume centroid to the back-projected source S (97 deg) disagrees with the ERA5 upwind azimuth (76 deg) by ~20 deg. This is within the 2-sigma wedge but widens source-localization uncertainty; sub-field placement should not be over-trusted.
counter_considerations: No specific facility can be identified — OGIM has zero wells/compressors/processing in Turkmenistan; this is field-level attribution only.
Sub-field placement is weakly constrained (see the 20 deg bearing gap and the speed-derived wedge); H2 cannot be excluded.
hypotheses.falsification: A facility-resolved inventory (or higher-resolution back-projection) placing the source outside BARSAGELMEZ, or evidence the plume originates from a non-O&G process, would falsify this.

candidate.descriptor: A different O&G source within BARSAGELMEZ or the adjacent GOTURDEPE field

hypotheses.claim: The source may instead be elsewhere within BARSAGELMEZ or in the adjacent GOTURDEPE field — an alternative the source-localization uncertainty cannot exclude. Still O&G, lower confidence than H1.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.54, band low: a residual-uncertainty alternative, below H1 by spatial consistency.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: Requires displacing the true source from S (which is inside BARSAGELMEZ). The localization uncertainty — speed-derived wedge + ~20 deg bearing gap — permits an alternative location elsewhere in BARSAGELMEZ or in adjacent GOTURDEPE (S not contained in GOTURDEPE; ~23 km to its interior), but does not favor it.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: Still O&G (same sector prior as H1).

evidence.statement: The bearing from the plume centroid to S (97 deg) disagrees with the ERA5 upwind azimuth (76 deg) by ~20 deg, and the wedge half-angle is speed-approximated — so an alternative in-field/adjacent location is within uncertainty.
GOTURDEPE (OGIM_ID 2017939) is an adjacent OIL & GAS field; S is NOT inside it (~23 km to its interior point), so it is a weaker but non-excludable alternative.

assumptions: Same back-projection assumptions as H1; this hypothesis specifically leans on their uncertainty being non-negligible.

WEAKEST LINK: the wedge half-angle (14.5 deg at 1-sigma, 27.3 deg at 2-sigma) is approximated from the ERA5 wind SPEED 1-sigma (1.79 m/s) treated as an isotropic wind-vector uncertainty — NOT a measured wind-direction variance. Sub-field localization rests on this approximation.
counter_considerations: S is contained in BARSAGELMEZ, not GOTURDEPE — this alternative is disfavored spatially and exists only because localization is weak.
hypotheses.falsification: Tighter localization (measured wind-direction variance or plume-resolved inversion) confirming S well inside BARSAGELMEZ would further demote this.

candidate.descriptor: Non-O&G source (natural geologic seep or other sector)

hypotheses.claim: A non-O&G origin (e.g. a natural geologic methane seep) is possible but unlikely given the active-gas-field context; ranked down but not dismissed.
hypotheses.confidence_rationale: Heuristic score 0.40, band low: stated for completeness; the active-field context strongly disfavors it but it is not excluded.

score_components.name: spatial_consistency
score_components.rationale: The back-projected location does not discriminate sector — any source type at that point is equally consistent spatially — so spatial is set neutral; the discriminator against a non-O&G source is the type prior.

score_components.name: type_prior
score_components.rationale: Natural geologic methane seeps exist in the South Caspian region, but an isolated ~27 t/hr point source spatially coincident with an active gas field is far more consistent with O&G; non-O&G is not excluded but is a low prior.

score_components.name: magnitude_consistency
score_components.rationale: A ~27 t/hr natural point seep is possible but high; magnitude weakly disfavors it.

evidence.statement: The back-projected source lies inside an active O&G field, which argues against a non-O&G origin; however no in-situ measurement excludes a natural seep.

assumptions: Sector priors are heuristic, not site-measured; a non-O&G source cannot be excluded without isotopic or in-situ confirmation.
counter_considerations: No OGIM or other committed dataset evidences a natural seep at this location; this hypothesis is included for honesty, not because of positive evidence.
hypotheses.falsification: Isotopic (delta-13C) or in-situ sampling indicating thermogenic vs biogenic/geologic origin would resolve sector.

provenance.thorpe_reference: Thorpe et al. 2023, Sci. Adv. 9 eadh2391, doi:10.1126/sciadv.adh2391
